_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for the Module 5 Study Worksheet.
_meta.philosophy: Fill-in worksheet. Terms only — no definitions inline. Ch 10 only; no inheritance (Ch 11 dropped from IS2053).

intro.title: How to Use This Worksheet
intro.body_html: This worksheet covers everything you need for the Module 5 Exam—plus professional Python practices that will serve you well beyond this course. Print it and fill it in by hand, or use the Copy as Rich Text / Copy as Plain Text buttons to paste it into Word and type your answers. If you work through this honestly, you’ll be prepared. If you skip it, you’re gambling. Your call.

content.text: Classes are blueprints. Objects are instances built from those blueprints. This is the core of OOP.
A class is like a ___, while an object is like a ___ made from it.

items: Instantiation (how do you create an object?)

content.text: What’s the relationship between a class and its objects?
__init__ runs automatically when you create an object. self refers to that specific object.

items: Automatic call (when does Python call it?)
Automatic passing (what does Python do for you?)

content.html: class City: def __init__(self, name, population): self.name = name self.population = population austin = City('Austin', 1000000)

content.text: Why do methods need self as the first parameter?

sections.title: Section 3: Accessors, Mutators, and Data Hiding

content.text: Getters return attribute values. Setters change them. This controls access to object data.
Why use getters and setters instead of accessing attributes directly?
Trace object creation step by step. Track each instance’s state separately. And know what trips beginners up.

items: Use meaningful names (nouns for classes, verbs for methods)

content.text: If you can answer these, you understand the material. If not, go back and review.

items: What’s the difference between a class and an object?
Why is self the first parameter in every method?
What’s the difference between an accessor and a mutator?
Why use data hiding? What does the underscore convention mean?
What happens if you forget self.attribute and just use attribute in __init__?

closing.body_html: That’s everything. Object-oriented programming is how professional software is built. You’ve been building a game all semester. This module makes it professional.
